# halo2_base - Rust

Crate [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)
==============================================================================================================================================================

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#1-416)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

Expand description

Base library to build Halo2 circuits.

[Re-exports](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html#reexports)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use halo2_proofs_axiom as halo2_proofs;`

[Modules](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[gates](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/index.html "halo2_base::gates mod")

Module that contains the main API for creating and working with circuits.

[safe\_types](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/index.html "halo2_base::safe_types mod")

Module for SafeType which enforce value range and realted functions.

[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html "halo2_base::utils mod")

Utility functions for converting between different types of field elements.

[Structs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html#structs)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "halo2_base::AssignedValue struct")

Pointer containing cell value and location within [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
.

[Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "halo2_base::Context struct")

Represents a single thread of an execution trace.

[ContextCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html "halo2_base::ContextCell struct")

Pointer to the position of a cell at `offset` in an advice column within a [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 of `context_id`.

[Enums](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html#enums)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "halo2_base::QuantumCell enum")

Convenience Enum which abstracts the scenarios under a value is added to an advice column.

[Constants](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html#constants)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[SKIP\_FIRST\_PASS](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/constant.SKIP_FIRST_PASS.html "halo2_base::SKIP_FIRST_PASS constant")

Constant representing whether the Layouter calls `synthesize` once just to get region shape.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[FieldChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html "halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip trait")

Trait for common functionality for finite field chips. Primarily intended to emulate a “non-native” finite field using “native” values in a prime field `F`. Most functions are designed for the case when the non-native field is larger than the native field, but the trait can still be implemented and used in other cases.

[FieldExtConstructor](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldExtConstructor.html "halo2_ecc::fields::FieldExtConstructor trait")

#### fn [instrument](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/trait.Instrument.html#method.instrument)
(self, span: [Span](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/span/struct.Span.html "struct tracing::span::Span")
) -> [Instrumented](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/struct.Instrumented.html "struct tracing::instrument::Instrumented")
<Self>

Instruments this type with the provided [`Span`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/span/struct.Span.html "Span")
, returning an `Instrumented` wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/trait.Instrument.html#method.instrument)

### impl<T, U> [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<U> for Twhere  
    U: [From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "trait core::convert::From")
<T>,

const: [unstable](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/88674 "Tracking issue for const_convert")
 · [source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/convert/mod.rs.html#725)
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.CRTInteger.html#method.into)

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

[ProperCrtUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperCrtUint.html "halo2_ecc::bigint::ProperCrtUint struct")

Safe wrapper for representing a BigUint as a [`CRTInteger`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.CRTInteger.html "CRTInteger")
 whose underlying BigUint value is in `[0, 2^t)` where `t = truncation.limbs.len() * limb_bits`. This struct guarantees that\
\
[ProperUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperUint.html "halo2_ecc::bigint::ProperUint struct")\
\
Safe wrapper around a BigUint represented as a vector of limbs in **little endian**. The underlying BigUint is represented by sumi limbs\[i\] \* 2limb\_bits \* i\
\

# zkevm_keccak - Rust

Crate [zkevm\_keccak](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/index.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)
==================================================================================================================================================================

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/zkevm_keccak/lib.rs.html#1-11)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

Expand description

The zkEVM keccak circuit implementation, with some minor modifications Credit goes to https://github.com/privacy-scaling-explorations/zkevm-circuits/tree/main/zkevm-circuits/src/keccak\_circuit

[Re-exports](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/index.html#reexports)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use keccak_packed_multi::[KeccakCircuitConfig](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/keccak_packed_multi/struct.KeccakCircuitConfig.html "struct zkevm_keccak::keccak_packed_multi::KeccakCircuitConfig")  as KeccakConfig;`

[Modules](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/index.html#modules)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[keccak\_packed\_multi](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/keccak_packed_multi/index.html "zkevm_keccak::keccak_packed_multi mod")

Keccak packed multi

[util](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/util/index.html "zkevm_keccak::util mod")

Util Utility traits, functions used in the crate.

[Macros](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/index.html#macros)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[address](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/macro.address.html "zkevm_keccak::address macro")

Create an [`Address`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/util/eth_types/type.Address.html "Address")
 from a hex string. Panics on invalid input.

[impl\_expr](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/macro.impl_expr.html "zkevm_keccak::impl_expr macro")

Implementation trait `Expr` for type able to be casted to u64

[word](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/macro.word.html "zkevm_keccak::word macro")

Create a [`Word`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/util/eth_types/type.Word.html "Word")
 from a hex string. Panics on invalid input.

[word\_map](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/macro.word_map.html "zkevm_keccak::word_map macro")

Create a [`Word`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/util/eth_types/type.Word.html "Word")
 to [`Word`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/util/eth_types/type.Word.html "Word")
 HashMap from pairs of hex strings. Panics on invalid input.

[StrictEcPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/struct.StrictEcPoint.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::StrictEcPoint struct")

An elliptic curve point where it is easy to compare the x-coordinate of two points

[Enums](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/index.html#enums)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ComparableEcPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/enum.ComparableEcPoint.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ComparableEcPoint enum")

An elliptic curve point where the x-coordinate has already been constrained to be reduced or not. In the reduced case one can more optimally compare equality of x-coordinates.

[Functions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/index.html#functions)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[check\_is\_on\_curve](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.check_is_on_curve.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::check_is_on_curve fn")

Checks that `P` is indeed a point on the elliptic curve `C`.

[ec\_add\_unequal](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.ec_add_unequal.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ec_add_unequal fn")

If `is_strict = true`, then this function constrains that `P.x != Q.x`. If you are calling this with `is_strict = false`, you must ensure that `P.x != Q.x` by some external logic (such as a mathematical theorem).

[ec\_double](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.ec_double.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ec_double fn")

Assumptions

[ec\_double\_and\_add\_unequal](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.ec_double_and_add_unequal.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ec_double_and_add_unequal fn")

Implements: computing 2P + Q = P + Q + P for P = (x0, y0), Q = (x1, y1)

[ec\_select](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.ec_select.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ec_select fn")

[ec\_select\_by\_indicator](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.ec_select_by_indicator.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ec_select_by_indicator fn")

[ec\_select\_from\_bits](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.ec_select_from_bits.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ec_select_from_bits fn")

[ec\_sub\_strict](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.ec_sub_strict.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ec_sub_strict fn")

Constrains `P != -Q` but allows `P == Q`, in which case output is (0,0). For Weierstrass curves only.

[ec\_sub\_unequal](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fn.ec_sub_unequal.html "halo2_ecc::ecc::ec_sub_unequal fn")

If `is_strict = true`, then this function constrains that `P.x != Q.x`. If you are calling this with `is_strict = false`, you must ensure that `P.x != Q.x` by some external logic (such as a mathematical theorem).

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/ecc/mod.rs.html#61-65)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct StrictEcPoint<F: PrimeField, FC: FieldChip<F>> {
        pub x: FC::ReducedFieldPoint,
        pub y: FC::FieldPoint,
        /* private fields */
    }

Expand description

An elliptic curve point where it is easy to compare the x-coordinate of two points

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/struct.StrictEcPoint.html#fields)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/struct.StrictEcPoint.html#structfield.x)

Struct [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html#)

================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#110-116)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct AssignedValue<F: ScalarField> {
        pub value: Assigned<F>,
        pub cell: Option<ContextCell>,
    }

Expand description

Pointer containing cell value and location within [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
.

Note: Performs a copy of the value, should only be used when you are about to assign the value again elsewhere.

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html#fields)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html#structfield.value)
`value: Assigned<F>`

Value of the cell.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html#structfield.cell)
`cell: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option") <[ContextCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html "struct halo2_base::ContextCell") >`

[ContextCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html "ContextCell")
 pointer to the cell the value is assigned to within an advice column of a [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
.

### impl<T, U> [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<U> for Twhere  
    U: [From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "trait core::convert::From")
<T>,

const: [unstable](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/88674 "Tracking issue for const_convert")
 · [source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/convert/mod.rs.html#725)
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html#method.into)

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

 = T

#### fn [to\_owned](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/trait.ToOwned.html#tymethod.to_owned)
(&self) -> T

Creates owned data from borrowed data, usually by cloning. [Read more](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/trait.ToOwned.html#tymethod.to_owned)

# Fp12Chip in halo2_ecc::fields::fp12 - Rust

Struct [halo2\_ecc](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/index.html)
::[fields](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/index.html)
::[fp12](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/index.html)
::[Fp12Chip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12.rs.html#20-23)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct Fp12Chip<'a, F: PrimeField, FpChip: FieldChip<F>, Fp12, const XI_0: i64>(pub FieldVectorChip<'a, F, FpChip>, _);

Expand description

Represent Fp12 point as FqPoint with degree = 12 `Fp12 = Fp2[w] / (w^6 - u - xi)` This implementation assumes p = 3 (mod 4) in order for the polynomial u^2 + 1 to be irreducible over Fp; i.e., in order for -1 to not be a square (quadratic residue) in Fp This means we store an Fp12 point as `\sum_{i = 0}^6 (a_{i0} + a_{i1} * u) * w^i` This is encoded in an FqPoint of degree 12 as `(a_{00}, ..., a_{50}, a_{01}, ..., a_{51})`

Tuple Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#fields)

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bn254/final_exp.rs.html#110-117)

#### pub fn [cyclotomic\_compress](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#method.cyclotomic_compress)
(&self, a: &[FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>) -> [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>>

in = g0 + g2 w + g4 w^2 + g1 w^3 + g3 w^4 + g5 w^5 where g\_i = g\_i0 + g\_i1 \* u are elements of Fp2 out = Compress(in) = \[ g2, g3, g4, g5 \]

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bn254/final_exp.rs.html#131-202)

#### pub fn [cyclotomic\_decompress](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#method.cyclotomic_decompress)
(  
    &self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    compression: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>>  
) -> [FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>

Input:

*   `compression = [g2, g3, g4, g5]` where g\_i are proper elements of Fp2 Output:
*   `Decompress(compression) = g0 + g2 w + g4 w^2 + g1 w^3 + g3 w^4 + g5 w^5` where
*   All elements of output are proper elements of Fp2 and: c = XI0 + u if g2 != 0: g1 = (g5^2 \* c + 3 g4^2 - 2 g3)/(4g2) g0 = (2 g1^2 + g2 \* g5 - 3 g3\*g4) \* c + 1 if g2 = 0: g1 = (2 g4 \* g5)/g3 g0 = (2 g1^2 - 3 g3 \* g4) \* c + 1

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bn254/final_exp.rs.html#216-263)

#### pub fn [cyclotomic\_square](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#method.cyclotomic_square)
(  
    &self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,

 = <FpChip::FieldType>::NUM\_BITS

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)

#### type [UnsafeFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)
 = [FieldVector](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/vector/struct.FieldVector.html "struct halo2_ecc::fields::vector::FieldVector")
<<FpChip as [FieldChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html "trait halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip")
<F>>::[UnsafeFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::UnsafeFieldPoint")
\>

A representation of a field element that is used for intermediate computations. The representation can have “overflows” (e.g., overflow limbs or negative limbs). [Read more](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint)

#### type [FieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint)
 = [FieldVector](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/vector/struct.FieldVector.html "struct halo2_ecc::fields::vector::FieldVector")
<<FpChip as [FieldChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html "trait halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip")
<F>>::[FieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::FieldPoint")
\>

The “proper” representation of a field element. Allowed to be a non-unique representation of a field element (e.g., can be greater than modulus)

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#associatedtype.ReducedFieldPoint)

#### type [ReducedFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.ReducedFieldPoint)
 = [FieldVector](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/vector/struct.FieldVector.html "struct halo2_ecc::fields::vector::FieldVector")
<<FpChip as [FieldChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html "trait halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip")
<F>>::[ReducedFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.ReducedFieldPoint "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::ReducedFieldPoint")
\>

A proper representation of field elements that guarantees a unique representation of each field element. Typically this means Uints that are less than the modulus.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp12/struct.Fp12Chip.html#associatedtype.FieldType)

#### type [FieldType](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldType)
 = Fp12

A type implementing `Field` trait to help with witness generation (for example with inverse)

    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    fe: Self::[FieldType](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldType "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::FieldType")

 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

Expand description

Module for SafeType which enforce value range and realted functions.

[Re-exports](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/index.html#reexports)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use crate::gates::flex_gate::[GateInstructions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/trait.GateInstructions.html "trait halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::GateInstructions") ;`

`pub use crate::gates::range::[RangeChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html "struct halo2_base::gates::range::RangeChip") ;`

`pub use crate::gates::range::[RangeInstructions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html "trait halo2_base::gates::range::RangeInstructions") ;`

`pub use crate::utils::[ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "trait halo2_base::utils::ScalarField") ;`

`pub use crate::[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue") ;`

`pub use crate::[Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context") ;`

`pub use crate::[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell") ;`

`pub use crate::QuantumCell::[Constant](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell") ;`

`pub use crate::QuantumCell::[Existing](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell") ;`

`pub use crate::QuantumCell::[Witness](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell") ;`

[Structs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/index.html#structs)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[SafeType](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/struct.SafeType.html "halo2_base::safe_types::SafeType struct")

SafeType’s goal is to avoid out-of-range undefined behavior. When building circuits, it’s common to use mulitple AssignedValue to represent a logical varaible. For example, we might want to represent a hash with 32 AssignedValue where each AssignedValue represents 1 byte. However, the range of AssignedValue is much larger than 1 byte(0~255). If a circuit takes 32 AssignedValue as inputs and some of them are actually greater than 255, there could be some undefined behaviors. SafeType gurantees the value range of its owned AssignedValue. So circuits don’t need to do any extra value checking if they take SafeType as inputs. TOTAL\_BITS is the number of total bits of this type. BYTES\_PER\_ELE is the number of bytes of each element.

[SafeTypeChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/struct.SafeTypeChip.html "halo2_base::safe_types::SafeTypeChip struct")

Chip for SafeType

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bigint/big_is_zero.rs.html#1-53)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

[Functions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/big_is_zero/index.html#functions)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[assign](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/big_is_zero/fn.assign.html "halo2_ecc::bigint::big_is_zero::assign fn")

Given ProperUint `a`, returns 1 iff every limb of `a` is zero. Returns 0 otherwise.

[crt](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/big_is_zero/fn.crt.html "halo2_ecc::bigint::big_is_zero::crt fn")

Returns 0 or 1. Returns 1 iff the limbs of `a` are identically zero. This just calls [`assign`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/big_is_zero/fn.assign.html "assign")
 on the limbs.

[positive](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/big_is_zero/fn.positive.html "halo2_ecc::bigint::big_is_zero::positive fn")

Assumptions

# Context in halo2_base - Rust

Struct [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)
====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#133-174)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct Context<F: ScalarField> {
        pub context_id: usize,
        pub advice: Vec<Assigned<F>>,
        pub cells_to_lookup: Vec<AssignedValue<F>>,
        pub zero_cell: Option<AssignedValue<F>>,
        pub selector: Vec<bool>,
        pub advice_equality_constraints: Vec<(ContextCell, ContextCell)>,
        pub constant_equality_constraints: Vec<(F, ContextCell)>,
        /* private fields */
    }

Expand description

Represents a single thread of an execution trace.

*   We keep the naming [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
     for historical reasons.

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#fields)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#structfield.context_id)
`context_id: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

Identifier to reference cells from this [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#structfield.advice)
`advice: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <Assigned<F>>`

Single column of advice cells.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#structfield.cells_to_lookup)
`cells_to_lookup: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue") <F>>`

[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 tracking all cells that lookup is enabled for.

*   When there is more than 1 advice column all `advice` cells will be copied to a single lookup enabled column to perform lookups.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#structfield.zero_cell)
`zero_cell: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option") <[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue") <F>>`

Cell that represents the zero value as AssignedValue

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#structfield.selector)
`selector: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <[bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html) >`

[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 representing the selector column of this [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 accompanying each `advice` column

*   Assumed to have the same length as `advice`

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#structfield.advice_equality_constraints)
`advice_equality_constraints: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <([ContextCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html "struct halo2_base::ContextCell") , [ContextCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html "struct halo2_base::ContextCell") )>`

A [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 tracking equality constraints between pairs of [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 `advice` cells.

Assumes both `advice` cells are in the same [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#structfield.constant_equality_constraints)
`constant_equality_constraints: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <(F, [ContextCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html "struct halo2_base::ContextCell") )>`

A [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 tracking pairs equality constraints between Fixed values and [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 `advice` cells.

Assumes the constant and `advice` cell are in the same [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
.

Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#implementations)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#176-416)

### impl<F: [ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "trait halo2_base::utils::ScalarField")
\> [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#180-191)

#### pub fn [new](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.new)
(witness\_gen\_only: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
, context\_id: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> Self

Creates a new [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 with the given `context_id` and witness generation enabled/disabled by the `witness_gen_only` flag.

*   `witness_gen_only`: flag to determine whether public key generation or only witness generation is being performed.
*   `context_id`: identifier to reference advice cells from this [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
     later.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#194-196)

#### pub fn [witness\_gen\_only](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.witness_gen_only)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns the `witness_gen_only` flag of the [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#200-228)

#### pub fn [assign\_cell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.assign_cell)
(&mut self, input: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")
<F>>)

Pushes a [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell<F>")
 to the end of the `advice` column ([Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of advice cells) in this [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
.

*   `input`: the cell to be assigned.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#231-239)

#### pub fn [last](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.last)
(&self) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>>

Returns the [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "AssignedValue")
 of the last cell in the `advice` column of [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 or [None](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html#variant.None "None")
 if `advice` is empty

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#246-256)

#### pub fn [get](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.get)
(&self, offset: [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>

Returns the [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "AssignedValue")
 of the cell at the given `offset` in the `advice` column of [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")

*   `offset`: the offset of the cell to be fetched
    *   `offset` may be negative indexing from the end of the column (e.g., `-1` is the last cell)
*   Assumes `offset` is a valid index in `advice`;
    *   `0` <= `offset` < `advice.len()` (or `advice.len() + offset >= 0` if `offset` is negative)

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#262-266)

#### pub fn [constrain\_equal](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.constrain_equal)
(&mut self, a: &[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>, b: &[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>)

Creates an equality constraint between two `advice` cells.

*   `a`: the first `advice` cell to be constrained equal
*   `b`: the second `advice` cell to be constrained equal
*   Assumes both cells are `advice` cells

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#273-298)

#### pub fn [assign\_region](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.assign_region)
<Q>(  
    &mut self,  
    inputs: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = Q>,  
    gate\_offsets: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)
\>  
)where  
    Q: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")
<F>>,

Pushes multiple advice cells to the `advice` column of [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 and enables them by enabling the corresponding selector specified in `gate_offset`.

*   `inputs`: Iterator that specifies the cells to be assigned
*   `gate_offsets`: specifies relative offset from current position to enable selector for the gate (e.g., `0` is inputs\[0\]).
    *   `offset` may be negative indexing from the end of the column (e.g., `-1` is the last previously assigned cell)

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#308-318)

#### pub fn [assign\_region\_last](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.assign_region_last)
<Q>(  
    &mut self,  
    inputs: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = Q>,  
    gate\_offsets: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)
\>  
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>where  
    Q: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")
<F>>,

Pushes multiple advice cells to the `advice` column of [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 and enables them by enabling the corresponding selector specified in `gate_offset` and returns the last assigned cell.

Assumes `gate_offsets` is the same length as `inputs`

Returns the last assigned cell

*   `inputs`: Iterator that specifies the cells to be assigned
*   `gate_offsets`: specifies indices to enable selector for the gate; assume `gate_offsets` is sorted in increasing order
    *   `offset` may be negative indexing from the end of the column (e.g., `-1` is the last cell)

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#327-366)

#### pub fn [assign\_region\_smart](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.assign_region_smart)
<Q>(  
    &mut self,  
    inputs: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = Q>,  
    gate\_offsets: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)
\>,  
    equality\_offsets: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = ([isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)
, [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)
)>,  
    external\_equality: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = ([Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[ContextCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html "struct halo2_base::ContextCell")
\>, [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)
)>  
)where  
    Q: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")
<F>>,

Pushes multiple advice cells to the `advice` column of [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 and enables them by enabling the corresponding selector specified in `gate_offset`.

Allows for the specification of equality constraints between cells at `equality_offsets` within the `advice` column and external advice cells specified in `external_equality` (e.g, Fixed column).

*   `gate_offsets`: specifies indices to enable selector for the gate;
    *   `offset` may be negative indexing from the end of the column (e.g., `-1` is the last cell)
*   `equality_offsets`: specifies pairs of indices to constrain equality
*   `external_equality`: specifies an existing cell to constrain equality with the cell at a certain index

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#370-385)

#### pub fn [assign\_witnesses](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.assign_witnesses)
(  
    &mut self,  
    witnesses: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")
<Item = F>  
) -> [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>>

Assigns a region of witness cells in an iterator and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of assigned cells.

*   `witnesses`: Iterator that specifies the cells to be assigned

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#389-395)

<F>

Assigns a witness value and returns the corresponding assigned cell.

*   `witness`: the witness value to be assigned

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#399-405)

#### pub fn [load\_constant](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.load_constant)

<F>

*   `c`: the constant value to be assigned

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#408-415)

#### pub fn [load\_zero](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html#method.load_zero)
(&mut self) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>

Assigns the 0 value to a new cell or returns a previously assigned zero cell from `zero_cell`.

 to this type, returning a [`WithDispatch`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "WithDispatch")
 wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_subscriber)

<Self>

Attaches the current [default](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/dispatcher/index.html#setting-the-default-subscriber)
 [`Subscriber`](super::Subscriber)
 to this type, returning a [`WithDispatch`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "WithDispatch")
 wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_current_subscriber)

# Fp2Chip in halo2_ecc::fields::fp2 - Rust

Struct [halo2\_ecc](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/index.html)
::[fields](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/index.html)
::[fp2](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp2/index.html)
::[Fp2Chip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp2/struct.Fp2Chip.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/fields/fp2.rs.html#19-22)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct Fp2Chip<'a, F: PrimeField, FpChip: FieldChip<F>, Fp2>(pub FieldVectorChip<'a, F, FpChip>, _);

Expand description

Represent Fp2 point as `FieldVector` with degree = 2 `Fp2 = Fp[u] / (u^2 + 1)` This implementation assumes p = 3 (mod 4) in order for the polynomial u^2 + 1 to be irreducible over Fp; i.e., in order for -1 to not be a square (quadratic residue) in Fp This means we store an Fp2 point as `a_0 + a_1 * u` where `a_0, a_1 in Fp`

User must construct an `FpChip` first using a config. This is intended so everything shares a single `FlexGateChip`, which is needed for the column allocation to work.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp2/struct.Fp2Chip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)

#### type [UnsafeFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)
 = [FieldVector](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/vector/struct.FieldVector.html "struct halo2_ecc::fields::vector::FieldVector")
<<FpChip as [FieldChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html "trait halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip")
<F>>::[UnsafeFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::UnsafeFieldPoint")
\>

A representation of a field element that is used for intermediate computations. The representation can have “overflows” (e.g., overflow limbs or negative limbs). [Read more](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp2/struct.Fp2Chip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint)

#### type [FieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint)
 = [FieldVector](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/vector/struct.FieldVector.html "struct halo2_ecc::fields::vector::FieldVector")
<<FpChip as [FieldChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html "trait halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip")
<F>>::[FieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::FieldPoint")
\>

The “proper” representation of a field element. Allowed to be a non-unique representation of a field element (e.g., can be greater than modulus)

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp2/struct.Fp2Chip.html#associatedtype.ReducedFieldPoint)

#### type [ReducedFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.ReducedFieldPoint)
 = [FieldVector](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/vector/struct.FieldVector.html "struct halo2_ecc::fields::vector::FieldVector")
<<FpChip as [FieldChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html "trait halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip")
<F>>::[ReducedFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.ReducedFieldPoint "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::ReducedFieldPoint")
\>

A proper representation of field elements that guarantees a unique representation of each field element. Typically this means Uints that are less than the modulus.

# halo2_base::utils - Rust

Module [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)
===============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#1-542)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

Expand description

Utility functions for converting between different types of field elements.

[Modules](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[fs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fs/index.html "halo2_base::utils::fs mod")

Module for reading parameters for Halo2 proving system from the file system.

[testing](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/testing/index.html "halo2_base::utils::testing mod")

Utilities for testing

[Traits](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html#traits)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[CurveAffineExt](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.CurveAffineExt.html "halo2_base::utils::CurveAffineExt trait")

[ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "halo2_base::utils::ScalarField trait")

Helper trait to represent a field element that can be converted into [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html "u64")
 limbs.

[Functions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html#functions)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[bigint\_to\_fe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.bigint_to_fe.html "halo2_base::utils::bigint_to_fe fn")

Converts an immutable reference to [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
 to a [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
.

[biguint\_to\_fe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.biguint_to_fe.html "halo2_base::utils::biguint_to_fe fn")

Converts an immutable reference to [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "BigUint")
 to a [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
.

[bit\_length](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.bit_length.html "halo2_base::utils::bit_length fn")

Returns the number of bits needed to represent the value of `x`.

[compose](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.compose.html "halo2_base::utils::compose fn")

Computes the value of an integer by passing as `input` a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of its limb values and the `bit_len` (bit length) used.

[decompose](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose.html "halo2_base::utils::decompose fn")

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 element into `number_of_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 represented by those limbs.

[decompose\_bigint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose_bigint.html "halo2_base::utils::decompose_bigint fn")

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
 into `num_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 represented by those limbs.

[decompose\_bigint\_option](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose_bigint_option.html "halo2_base::utils::decompose_bigint_option fn")

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
 into `num_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 represented by those limbs wrapped in \[Value\].

[decompose\_biguint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose_biguint.html "halo2_base::utils::decompose_biguint fn")

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "BigUint")
 into `num_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 represented by those limbs.

[decompose\_fe\_to\_u64\_limbs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose_fe_to_u64_limbs.html "halo2_base::utils::decompose_fe_to_u64_limbs fn")

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "ScalarField")
 element into `number_of_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html "u64")
 represented by those limbs.

[fe\_to\_bigint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.fe_to_bigint.html "halo2_base::utils::fe_to_bigint fn")

Converts a [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 element into a [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
 element by sending `fe` in `[0, F::modulus())` to\
\
[fe\_to\_biguint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.fe_to_biguint.html "halo2_base::utils::fe_to_biguint fn")\
\
Converts an immutable reference to an PrimeField element into a [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "BigUint")\
 element.\
\
[log2\_ceil](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.log2_ceil.html "halo2_base::utils::log2_ceil fn")\
\
Returns the ceiling of the base 2 logarithm of `x`.\
\
[modulus](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.modulus.html "halo2_base::utils::modulus fn")\
\
Returns the modulus of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")\
.\
\
[power\_of\_two](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.power_of_two.html "halo2_base::utils::power_of_two fn")\
\
Returns the [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")\
 element of 2n.\
\
[value\_to\_option](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.value_to_option.html "halo2_base::utils::value_to_option fn")\
\
Wraps the internal value of `value` in an [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "Option")\
. If the value is [None](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html#variant.None "None")\
, then the function returns [None](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html#variant.None "None")\
.\
\
[Trait Aliases](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html#trait-aliases)\
\
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\
\

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/fields/fp.rs.html#304-322)
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#method.range_check)

#### fn [range\_check](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#tymethod.range_check)
(  
    &self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[CRTInteger](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.CRTInteger.html "struct halo2_ecc::bigint::CRTInteger")
<F>>,  
    max\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
  
)

##### [Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#assumptions)

*   `max_bits <= n * k` where `n = self.limb_bits` and `k = self.num_limbs`
*   `a.truncation.limbs.len() = self.num_limbs`

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/fields/fp.rs.html#334-341)
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#method.is_soft_zero)

#### fn [is\_soft\_zero](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#tymethod.is_soft_zero)
(  
    &self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[ProperCrtUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperCrtUint.html "struct halo2_ecc::bigint::ProperCrtUint")
<F>>  
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>

Returns 1 iff `a` is 0 as a BigUint. This means that even if `a` is 0 modulo `p`, this may return 0.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/fields/fp.rs.html#347-364)
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#method.is_soft_nonzero)

#### fn [is\_soft\_nonzero](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#tymethod.is_soft_nonzero)
(  
    &self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[ProperCrtUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperCrtUint.html "struct halo2_ecc::bigint::ProperCrtUint")
<F>>  
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>

Given proper CRT integer `a`, returns 1 iff `a < modulus::<F>()` and `a != 0` as integers

##### [Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#assumptions-1)

*   `a` is proper representation of BigUint

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/fields/fp.rs.html#149)
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#associatedconstant.PRIME_FIELD_NUM_BITS)

#### const [PRIME\_FIELD\_NUM\_BITS](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedconstant.PRIME_FIELD_NUM_BITS)
: [u32](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u32.html)
 = Fp::NUM\_BITS

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)

#### type [UnsafeFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)
 = [CRTInteger](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.CRTInteger.html "struct halo2_ecc::bigint::CRTInteger")
<F>

A representation of a field element that is used for intermediate computations. The representation can have “overflows” (e.g., overflow limbs or negative limbs). [Read more](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.UnsafeFieldPoint)

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint)

#### type [FieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint)
 = [ProperCrtUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperCrtUint.html "struct halo2_ecc::bigint::ProperCrtUint")
<F>

The “proper” representation of a field element. Allowed to be a non-unique representation of a field element (e.g., can be greater than modulus)

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#associatedtype.ReducedFieldPoint)

#### type [ReducedFieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.ReducedFieldPoint)
 = [Reduced](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.Reduced.html "struct halo2_ecc::fields::fp::Reduced")
<[ProperCrtUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperCrtUint.html "struct halo2_ecc::bigint::ProperCrtUint")
<F>, Fp>

A proper representation of field elements that guarantees a unique representation of each field element. Typically this means Uints that are less than the modulus.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html#associatedtype.FieldType)

#### type [FieldType](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldType)
 = Fp

A type implementing `Field` trait to help with witness generation (for example with inverse)

(&self, ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")

<F>

Assigns `fe` as private witness. Note that the witness may **not** be constrained to be a unique representation of the field element `fe`.

============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#99-104)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct ContextCell {
        pub context_id: usize,
        pub offset: usize,
    }

Expand description

Pointer to the position of a cell at `offset` in an advice column within a [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 of `context_id`.

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html#fields)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html#structfield.context_id)
`context_id: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

Identifier of the [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 that this cell belongs to.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.ContextCell.html#structfield.offset)
`offset: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

Relative offset of the cell within this [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 advice column.

    limbs: impl [IntoIterator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/iter/traits/collect/trait.IntoIterator.html "trait core::iter::traits::collect::IntoIterator")

) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>

Computes `sum_i limbs[i] * limb_bases[i]` in native field `F`. In practice assumes `limb_bases[i] = 2^{limb_bits * i}`.

# ProperUint in halo2_ecc::bigint - Rust

Struct [halo2\_ecc](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/index.html)
::[bigint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/index.html)
::[ProperUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperUint.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bigint/mod.rs.html#84)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    #[repr(transparent)]pub struct ProperUint<F: ScalarField>(_);

Expand description

Safe wrapper around a BigUint represented as a vector of limbs in **little endian**. The underlying BigUint is represented by sumi limbs\[i\] \* 2limb\_bits \* i

To save memory we do not store the `limb_bits` and it must be inferred from context.

Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperUint.html#implementations)

#### pub fn [into\_crt](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperUint.html#method.into_crt)
(  
    self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    gate: &impl [GateInstructions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/trait.GateInstructions.html "trait halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::GateInstructions")
<F>,  
    value: [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "struct num_bigint::biguint::BigUint")
,  
    limb\_bases: &[\[F\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.slice.html)
,  
    limb\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
  
) -> [ProperCrtUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperCrtUint.html "struct halo2_ecc::bigint::ProperCrtUint")
<F>

Computes `sum_i limbs[i] * limb_bases[i]` in native field `F`. In practice assumes `limb_bases[i] = 2^{limb_bits * i}`.

Assumes that `value` is the underlying BigUint value represented by `self`.

Trait Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperUint.html#trait-implementations)

![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/fields/fp.rs.html#30)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct Reduced<FieldPoint, Fp>(_, _);

Expand description

Wrapper around `FieldPoint` to guarantee this is a “reduced” representation of an `Fp` field element. A reduced representation guarantees that there is a _unique_ representation of each field element. Typically this means Uints that are less than the modulus.

Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.Reduced.html#implementations)

##### [Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.Fp12Chip.html#assumptions)

*   `a` is nonzero field point

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bn254/final_exp.rs.html#110-117)

#### pub fn [cyclotomic\_compress](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.Fp12Chip.html#method.cyclotomic_compress)
(&self, a: &[FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>) -> [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>>

in = g0 + g2 w + g4 w^2 + g1 w^3 + g3 w^4 + g5 w^5 where g\_i = g\_i0 + g\_i1 \* u are elements of Fp2 out = Compress(in) = \[ g2, g3, g4, g5 \]

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bn254/final_exp.rs.html#131-202)

#### pub fn [cyclotomic\_decompress](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.Fp12Chip.html#method.cyclotomic_decompress)
(  
    &self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    compression: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>>  
) -> [FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>

Input:

*   `compression = [g2, g3, g4, g5]` where g\_i are proper elements of Fp2 Output:
*   `Decompress(compression) = g0 + g2 w + g4 w^2 + g1 w^3 + g3 w^4 + g5 w^5` where
*   All elements of output are proper elements of Fp2 and: c = XI0 + u if g2 != 0: g1 = (g5^2 \* c + 3 g4^2 - 2 g3)/(4g2) g0 = (2 g1^2 + g2 \* g5 - 3 g3\*g4) \* c + 1 if g2 = 0: g1 = (2 g4 \* g5)/g3 g0 = (2 g1^2 - 3 g3 \* g4) \* c + 1

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bn254/final_exp.rs.html#216-263)

#### pub fn [cyclotomic\_square](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.Fp12Chip.html#method.cyclotomic_square)
(  
    &self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    compression: &\[[FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")\
<F>\]  
) -> [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[FqPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.FqPoint.html "type halo2_ecc::bn254::FqPoint")
<F>>

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bn254/final_exp.rs.html#268-301)

#### pub fn [cyclotomic\_pow](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.Fp12Chip.html#method.cyclotomic_pow)

<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
\>

<F>

##### [Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bn254/type.Fp12Chip.html#assumptions-1)

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bn254/final_exp.rs.html#305-370)

# QuantumCell in halo2_base - Rust

Enum [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)
========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/lib.rs.html#58-72)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub enum QuantumCell<F: ScalarField> {
        Existing(AssignedValue<F>),
        Witness(F),
        WitnessFraction(Assigned<F>),
        Constant(F),
    }

Expand description

Convenience Enum which abstracts the scenarios under a value is added to an advice column.

Variants[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html#variants)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html#variant.Existing)
`Existing([AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue") <F>)`

An [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "AssignedValue")
 already existing in the advice column (e.g., a witness value that was already assigned in a previous cell in the column).

*   Assigns a new cell into the advice column with value equal to the value of a.
*   Imposes an equality constraint between the new cell and the cell of a so the Verifier guarantees that these two cells are always equal.

### [](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html#variant.Witness)
`Witness(F)`

A non-existing witness [ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "ScalarField")
 value (e.g. private input) to add to an advice column.

### [](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html#variant.WitnessFraction)
`WitnessFraction(Assigned<F>)`

A non-existing witness [ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "ScalarField")
 marked as a fraction for optimization in batch inversion later.

### [](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html#variant.Constant)
`Constant(F)`

A known constant value added as a witness value to the advice column and added to the “Fixed” column during circuit creation time.

*   Visible to both the Prover and the Verifier.
*   Imposes an equality constraint between the two corresponding cells in the advice and fixed columns.

Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html#implementations)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

(&self) -> [&](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
F

 value of a QuantumCell.

Panics if the QuantumCell is of type WitnessFraction.

# ProperCrtUint in halo2_ecc::bigint - Rust

Struct [halo2\_ecc](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/index.html)
::[bigint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/index.html)
::[ProperCrtUint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperCrtUint.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/bigint/mod.rs.html#220)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    #[repr(transparent)]pub struct ProperCrtUint<F: ScalarField>(_);

Expand description

Safe wrapper for representing a BigUint as a [`CRTInteger`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.CRTInteger.html "CRTInteger")
 whose underlying BigUint value is in `[0, 2^t)` where `t = truncation.limbs.len() * limb_bits`. This struct guarantees that\
\
*   each `truncation.limbs[i]` is ranged checked to be in `[0, 2^limb_bits)`,\
*   `native` is the evaluation of `sum_i truncation.limbs[i] * 2^{limb_bits * i} (mod modulus::<F>)` in the native field `F`\
*   `value` is equal to `sum_i truncation.limbs[i] * 2^{limb_bits * i}` as integers\
\
Note this means `native` and `value` are completely determined by `truncation`. However, we still store them explicitly for convenience.\
\
Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/bigint/struct.ProperCrtUint.html#implementations)\
\
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\
\

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

Expand description

Module that contains the main API for creating and working with circuits.

[Re-exports](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/index.html#reexports)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use flex_gate::[GateChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.GateChip.html "struct halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::GateChip") ;`

<F, FC::[FieldPoint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldPoint "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::FieldPoint")
\>where  
    C: CurveAffineExt<Base = FC::[FieldType](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/trait.FieldChip.html#associatedtype.FieldType "type halo2_ecc::fields::FieldChip::FieldType")
\>,

None of elements in `points` can be point at infinity.

The user should filter out base points that are identity beforehand; we do not separately do this here

[FpChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.FpChip.html "halo2_ecc::fields::fp::FpChip struct")

[Reduced](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/fields/fp/struct.Reduced.html "halo2_ecc::fields::fp::Reduced struct")

Wrapper around `FieldPoint` to guarantee this is a “reduced” representation of an `Fp` field element. A reduced representation guarantees that there is a _unique_ representation of each field element. Typically this means Uints that are less than the modulus.
