::[msm\_par](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fixed_base/fn.msm_par.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/ecc/fixed_base.rs.html#112-219)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn msm_par<F, FC, C>(    chip: &EccChip<'_, F, FC>,    builder: &mut GateThreadBuilder<F>,    points: &[C],    scalars: Vec<Vec<AssignedValue<F>>>,    max_scalar_bits_per_cell: usize,    window_bits: usize,    phase: usize) -> EcPoint<F, FC::FieldPoint>where    F: PrimeField,    C: CurveAffineExt,    FC: FieldChip<F, FieldType = C::Base> + Selectable<F, FC::FieldPoint>,

Expand description

[Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fixed_base/fn.msm_par.html#assumptions)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `points.len() = scalars.len()`
*   `scalars[i].len() = scalars[j].len()` for all `i,j`
*   `points` are all on the curve
*   `points[i]` is not point at infinity (0, 0); these should be filtered out beforehand
*   The integer value of `scalars[i]` is less than the order of `points[i]`
*   Output may be point at infinity, in which case (0, 0) is returned

---

# scalar_multiply in halo2_ecc::ecc::fixed_base - Rust

Function [halo2\_ecc](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/index.html)
::[ecc](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/index.html)
::[fixed\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fixed_base/index.html)
::[scalar\_multiply](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fixed_base/fn.scalar_multiply.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/ecc/fixed_base.rs.html#21-100)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn scalar_multiply<F, FC, C>(    chip: &FC,    ctx: &mut Context<F>,    point: &C,    scalar: Vec<AssignedValue<F>>,    max_bits: usize,    window_bits: usize) -> EcPoint<F, FC::FieldPoint>where    F: PrimeField,    C: CurveAffineExt,    FC: FieldChip<F, FieldType = C::Base> + Selectable<F, FC::FieldPoint>,

Expand description

Computes `[scalar] * P` on y^2 = x^3 + b where `P` is fixed (constant)

*   `scalar` is represented as a non-empty reference array of `AssignedValue`s
*   `scalar = sum_i scalar_i * 2^{max_bits * i}`
*   an array of length > 1 is needed when `scalar` exceeds the modulus of scalar field `F`

[Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/fixed_base/fn.scalar_multiply.html#assumptions)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `scalar_i < 2^{max_bits} for all i` (constrained by num\_to\_bits)
*   `scalar > 0`
*   `max_bits <= modulus::<F>.bits()`

---

Expand description

multiply Fp2 elts: (a0 + a1 \* u) \* (XI0 + u) without carry

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `a` is `Fp2` point represented as `FieldVector` with degree = 2

Function [halo2\_ecc](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/index.html)
::[ecc](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/index.html)

::[multi\_exp\_par](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/pippenger/fn.multi_exp_par.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_ecc/ecc/pippenger.rs.html#219-341)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn multi_exp_par<F: PrimeField, FC, C>(    chip: &FC,    builder: &mut GateThreadBuilder<F>,    points: &[EcPoint<F, FC::FieldPoint>],    scalars: Vec<Vec<AssignedValue<F>>>,    max_scalar_bits_per_cell: usize,    clump_factor: usize,    phase: usize) -> EcPoint<F, FC::FieldPoint>where    FC: FieldChip<F> + Selectable<F, FC::FieldPoint> + Selectable<F, FC::ReducedFieldPoint>,    C: CurveAffineExt<Base = FC::FieldType>,

Expand description

Multi-thread witness generation for multi-scalar multiplication.

[Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_ecc/ecc/pippenger/fn.multi_exp_par.html#assumptions)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `points.len() == scalars.len()`
*   `scalars[i].len() == scalars[j].len()` for all `i, j`
*   `points` are all on the curve or the point at infinity
*   `points[i]` is allowed to be (0, 0) to represent the point at infinity (identity point)
*   Currently implementation assumes that the only point on curve with y-coordinate equal to `0` is identity point

---

\
Pack bits stored in a u64 value into a sparse keccak word\

\
Pack bits in the range \[0,BIT\_SIZE\[ into a sparse keccak word with the specified bit base\
\

\
Rotates a word that was split into parts to the right\

[rotate\_left](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/zkevm_keccak/util/fn.rotate_left.html "zkevm_keccak::util::rotate_left fn")\
\

\
Rotates a word that was split into parts to the left\
\

\
Returns the size (in bits) of each part size when splitting up a keccak word in parts of `part_size`\
\

Expand description

Module for reading parameters for Halo2 proving system from the file system.

[Functions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fs/index.html#functions)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[gen\_srs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fs/fn.gen_srs.html "halo2_base::utils::fs::gen_srs fn")

Generates the SRS for the KZG scheme and writes it to a file found in “./params/kzg\_bn2\_{k}.srs`or`{dir}/kzg\_bn254\_{k}.srs`if`PARAMS\_DIR\` env var is specified, creates a file it if it does not exist”

[read\_or\_create\_srs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fs/fn.read_or_create_srs.html "halo2_base::utils::fs::read_or_create_srs fn")

Attempts to read the srs from a file found in `./params/kzg_bn254_{k}.srs` or `{dir}/kzg_bn254_{k}.srs` if `PARAMS_DIR` env var is specified, creates a file it if it does not exist.

[read\_params](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fs/fn.read_params.html "halo2_base::utils::fs::read_params fn")

Reads the srs from a file found in `./params/kzg_bn254_{k}.srs` or `{dir}/kzg_bn254_{k}.srs` if `PARAMS_DIR` env var is specified.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#433-447)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct RangeChip<F: ScalarField> {
        pub gate: GateChip<F>,
        pub lookup_bits: usize,
        pub limb_bases: Vec<QuantumCell<F>>,
        /* private fields */
    }

Expand description

A chip that implements RangeInstructions which provides methods to constrain a field element `x` is within a range of bits.

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#fields)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#structfield.gate)
`gate: [GateChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.GateChip.html "struct halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::GateChip") <F>`

Underlying [GateChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.GateChip.html "GateChip")
 for this chip.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#structfield.lookup_bits)
`lookup_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

Defines the number of bits represented in the lookup table \[0,2lookup\_bits).\
\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#structfield.limb_bases)\
`limb_bases: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell") <F>>`\
\
[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")\
 of powers of `2 ** lookup_bits` represented as [QuantumCell::Constant](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html#variant.Constant "QuantumCell::Constant")\
. These are precomputed and cached as a performance optimization for later limb decompositions. We precompute up to the higher power that fits in `F`, which is `2 ** ((F::CAPACITY / lookup_bits) * lookup_bits)`.\
\
Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#implementations)\
\
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#449-475)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#impl-RangeChip%3CF%3E)\
\

\

\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#486-488)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.strategy)\
\
#### fn [strategy](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#tymethod.strategy)\

\

 for this range.\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#491-493)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.lookup_bits)\
\
#### fn [lookup\_bits](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#tymethod.lookup_bits)\
(&self) -> [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
\
Defines the number of bits represented in the lookup table \[0,2lookup\_bits).\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#507-552)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.range_check)\
\
#### fn [range\_check](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#tymethod.range_check)\
(&self, ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>, a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>, range\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
)\
\
Checks and constrains that `a` lies in the range \[0, 2range\_bits).\
\
This is done by decomposing `a` into `k` limbs, where `k = ceil(range_bits / lookup_bits)`. Each limb is constrained to be within the range \[0, 2lookup\_bits). The limbs are then combined to form `a` again with the last limb having `rem_bits` number of bits.\
\
*   `a`: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "AssignedValue")\
     value to be range checked\
*   `range_bits`: number of bits in the range\
*   `lookup_bits`: number of bits in the lookup table\
\
##### [Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#assumptions)\
\
*   `ceil(range_bits / lookup_bits) * lookup_bits <= F::CAPACITY`\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#562-591)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.check_less_than)\
\
#### fn [check\_less\_than](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#tymethod.check_less_than)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    b: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    num\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
  \
)\
\
Constrains that ‘a’ is less than ‘b’.\
\
Assumes that`a` and `b` are known to have <= num\_bits bits.\
\
Note: This may fail silently if a or b have more than num\_bits\
\
*   a: [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     value to check\
*   b: upper bound expressed as a [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
    \
*   num\_bits: number of bits to represent the values\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#602-645)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.is_less_than)\
\
#### fn [is\_less\_than](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#tymethod.is_less_than)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    b: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    num\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
  \
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>\
\
Constrains whether `a` is in `[0, b)`, and returns 1 if `a` < `b`, otherwise 0.\
\
*   a: first [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     to compare\
*   b: second [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     to compare\
*   num\_bits: number of bits to represent the values\
\
##### [Assumptions](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#assumptions-1)\
\
*   `a` and `b` are known to have `<= num_bits` bits.\
*   (`ceil(num_bits / lookup_bits) + 1) * lookup_bits <= F::CAPACITY`\
\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#associatedtype.Gate)\
\
#### type [Gate](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#associatedtype.Gate)\
 = [GateChip](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.GateChip.html "struct halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::GateChip")\
<F>\
\
The type of Gate used within the instructions.\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#225-231)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.check_less_than_safe)\
\
#### fn [check\_less\_than\_safe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.check_less_than_safe)\
(&self, ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>, a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>, b: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)\
)\
\
Performs a range check that `a` has at most `bit_length(b)` bits and then constrains that `a` is less than `b`. [Read more](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.check_less_than_safe)\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#237-246)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.check_big_less_than_safe)\
\
#### fn [check\_big\_less\_than\_safe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.check_big_less_than_safe)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>,  \
    b: [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "struct num_bigint::biguint::BigUint")\
  \
)where  \
    F: [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "traitalias halo2_base::utils::BigPrimeField")\
,\
\
Performs a range check that `a` has at most `bit_length(b)` bits and then constrains that `a` is less than `b`. [Read more](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.check_big_less_than_safe)\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#268-279)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.is_less_than_safe)\
\
#### fn [is\_less\_than\_safe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.is_less_than_safe)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>,  \
    b: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)\
  \
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>\
\
Performs a range check that `a` has at most `ceil(bit_length(b) / lookup_bits) * lookup_bits` and then constrains that `a` is in `[0,b)`. [Read more](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.is_less_than_safe)\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#289-303)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.is_big_less_than_safe)\
\
#### fn [is\_big\_less\_than\_safe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.is_big_less_than_safe)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>,  \
    b: [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "struct num_bigint::biguint::BigUint")\
  \
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>where  \
    F: [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "traitalias halo2_base::utils::BigPrimeField")\
,\
\
Performs a range check that `a` has at most `ceil(b.bits() / lookup_bits) * lookup_bits` bits and then constrains that `a` is in `[0,b)`. [Read more](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.is_big_less_than_safe)\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#311-338)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.div_mod)\
\
#### fn [div\_mod](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.div_mod)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    b: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "struct num_bigint::biguint::BigUint")\
\>,  \
    a\_num\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
  \
) -> ([AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>, [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>)where  \
    F: [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "traitalias halo2_base::utils::BigPrimeField")\
,\
\
Constrains and returns `(c, r)` such that `a = b * c + r`. [Read more](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.div_mod)\
\

#### fn [instrument](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/trait.Instrument.html#method.instrument)\

, returning an `Instrumented` wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.37/tracing/instrument/trait.Instrument.html#method.instrument)\
\

### impl<T, U> [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<U> for Twhere  \
    U: [From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "trait core::convert::From")\
<T>,\
\
const: [unstable](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/88674 "Tracking issue for const_convert")\
 · [source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/convert/mod.rs.html#725)\
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeChip.html#method.into)\
\
#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)\
(self) -> U\
\
Calls `U::from(self)`.\
\
That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "From") <T> for U` chooses to do.\

# bigint_to_fe in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[bigint\_to\_fe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.bigint_to_fe.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#131-139)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn bigint_to_fe<F: BigPrimeField>(e: &BigInt) -> F

Expand description

Converts an immutable reference to [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
 to a [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
.

*   `e`: immutable reference to [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
    

[Assumptions:](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.bigint_to_fe.html#assumptions)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The absolute value of `e` is less than the modulus of `F`

---

Expand description

Trait that implements methods to constrain a field element number `x` is within a range of bits.

#### fn [range\_check](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#tymethod.range_check)
(&self, ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>, a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")

)

Checks and constrains that `a` lies in the range \[0, 2range\_bits).\
\
Assumes that both `a`<= `range_bits` bits.\
\
*   a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "AssignedValue")\
     value to be range checked\

#### fn [check\_less\_than](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#tymethod.check_less_than)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    b: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    num\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
  \
)\
\
Constrains that ‘a’ is less than ‘b’.\
\
Assumes that `a` and `b` have bit length <= num\_bits bits.\
\
Note: This may fail silently if a or b have more than num\_bits.\
\
*   a: [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     value to check\
*   b: upper bound expressed as a [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
    \
*   num\_bits: number of bits used to represent the values of `a` and `b`\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#254-260)\
\
#### fn [is\_less\_than](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#tymethod.is_less_than)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    b: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    num\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
  \
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>\
\
Constrains whether `a` is in `[0, b)`, and returns 1 if `a` < `b`, otherwise 0.\
\
Assumes that`a` and `b` are known to have <= num\_bits bits.\
\
*   a: first [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     to compare\
*   b: second [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     to compare\
*   num\_bits: number of bits to represent the values\
\

#### fn [check\_less\_than\_safe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.check_less_than_safe)\
(&self, ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\

#### fn [is\_less\_than\_safe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.is_less_than_safe)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>,  \
    b: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)\
  \
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>\
\
Performs a range check that `a` has at most `ceil(bit_length(b) / lookup_bits) * lookup_bits` and then constrains that `a` is in `[0,b)`.\
\
Returns 1 if `a` < `b`, otherwise 0.\
\
*   a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "AssignedValue")\
     value to check\
*   b: upper bound as [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html "u64")\
     value\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#289-303)\
\
#### fn [is\_big\_less\_than\_safe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.is_big_less_than_safe)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>,  \
    b: [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "struct num_bigint::biguint::BigUint")\
  \
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>where  \
    F: [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "traitalias halo2_base::utils::BigPrimeField")\
,\
\
Performs a range check that `a` has at most `ceil(b.bits() / lookup_bits) * lookup_bits` bits and then constrains that `a` is in `[0,b)`.\
\
Returns 1 if `a` < `b`, otherwise 0.\
\
*   a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "AssignedValue")\
     value to check\
*   b: upper bound as [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "BigUint")\
     value\
\
For the current implementation using \[`is_less_than`\], we require `ceil(b.bits() / lookup_bits) + 1 < F::NUM_BITS / lookup_bits`\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#311-338)\
\
#### fn [div\_mod](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.div_mod)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    b: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "struct num_bigint::biguint::BigUint")\
\>,  \
    a\_num\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
  \
) -> ([AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>, [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>)where  \
    F: [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "traitalias halo2_base::utils::BigPrimeField")\
,\
\
Constrains and returns `(c, r)` such that `a = b * c + r`.\
\
Assumes that `b != 0` and that `a` has <= `a_num_bits` bits.\
\
*   a: [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     value to divide\
*   b: [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "BigUint")\
     value to divide by\
*   a\_num\_bits: number of bits needed to represent the value of `a`\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#357-403)\
\
#### fn [div\_mod\_var](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.div_mod_var)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    b: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")\
<[QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "enum halo2_base::QuantumCell")\
<F>>,  \
    a\_num\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
,  \
    b\_num\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
  \
) -> ([AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>, [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>)where  \
    F: [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "traitalias halo2_base::utils::BigPrimeField")\
,\
\
Constrains and returns `(c, r)` such that `a = b * c + r`.\
\
Assumes: that `b != 0`. that `a` has <= `a_num_bits` bits. that `b` has <= `b_num_bits` bits.\
\
Note: Let `X = 2 ** b_num_bits` Write `a = a1 * X + a0` and `c = c1 * X + c0` If we write `b * c0 + r = d1 * X + d0` then `b * c + r = (b * c1 + d1) * X + d0`\
\
*   a: [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     value to divide\
*   b: [QuantumCell](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/enum.QuantumCell.html "QuantumCell")\
     value to divide by\
*   a\_num\_bits: number of bits needed to represent the value of `a`\
*   b\_num\_bits: number of bits needed to represent the value of `b`\
\
[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/range.rs.html#410-428)\
\
#### fn [get\_last\_bit](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/trait.RangeInstructions.html#method.get_last_bit)\
(  \
    &self,  \
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")\
<F>,  \
    a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>,  \
    limb\_bits: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
  \
) -> [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")\
<F>\
\
Constrains and returns the last bit of the value of `a`.\
\
Assume `a` has been range checked already to `limb_bits` bits.\
\
*   a: [AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "AssignedValue")\
     value to get the last bit of\
*   limb\_bits: number of bits in a limb\
\

# SafeType in halo2_base::safe_types - Rust

Struct [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[safe\_types](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/index.html)
::[SafeType](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/struct.SafeType.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/safe_types/mod.rs.html#30-33)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct SafeType<F: ScalarField, const BYTES_PER_ELE: usize, const TOTAL_BITS: usize> { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

SafeType’s goal is to avoid out-of-range undefined behavior. When building circuits, it’s common to use mulitple AssignedValue to represent a logical varaible. For example, we might want to represent a hash with 32 AssignedValue where each AssignedValue represents 1 byte. However, the range of AssignedValue is much larger than 1 byte(0~255). If a circuit takes 32 AssignedValue as inputs and some of them are actually greater than 255, there could be some undefined behaviors. SafeType gurantees the value range of its owned AssignedValue. So circuits don’t need to do any extra value checking if they take SafeType as inputs. TOTAL\_BITS is the number of total bits of this type. BYTES\_PER\_ELE is the number of bytes of each element.

Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/struct.SafeType.html#implementations)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# biguint_to_fe in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[biguint\_to\_fe](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.biguint_to_fe.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#121-124)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn biguint_to_fe<F: BigPrimeField>(e: &BigUint) -> F

Expand description

Converts an immutable reference to [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "BigUint")
 to a [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
.

*   `e`: immutable reference to [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "BigUint")
    

[Assumptions:](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.biguint_to_fe.html#assumptions)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `e` is less than the modulus of `F`

# compose in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[compose](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.compose.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#289-291)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn compose(input: Vec<BigUint>, bit_len: usize) -> BigUint

Expand description

Computes the value of an integer by passing as `input` a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of its limb values and the `bit_len` (bit length) used.

Returns the sum of all limbs scaled by 2(bit\_len \* i) where i is the index of the limb.

*   `input`: Limb values of the integer.
*   `bit_len`: Length of limb in bits

---

# decompose in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[decompose](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#169-175)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn decompose<F: BigPrimeField>(    e: &F,    number_of_limbs: usize,    bit_len: usize) -> Vec<F>

Expand description

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 element into `number_of_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 represented by those limbs.

Assumes `bit_len < 128`.

*   `e`: immutable reference to [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
     element to decompose
*   `number_of_limbs`: number of limbs to decompose `e` into
*   `bit_len`: number of bits in each limb

---

# decompose_bigint in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[decompose\_bigint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose_bigint.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#251-257)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn decompose_bigint<F: BigPrimeField>(    e: &BigInt,    num_limbs: usize,    bit_len: usize) -> Vec<F>

Expand description

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
 into `num_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 represented by those limbs.

Assumes `bit_len < 128`.

*   `e`: immutable reference to `BigInt` to decompose
*   `num_limbs`: number of limbs to decompose `e` into
*   `bit_len`: number of bits in each limb

---

#### pub fn [raw\_bytes\_to](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/struct.SafeTypeChip.html#method.raw_bytes_to)
<const BYTES\_PER\_ELE: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
, const TOTAL\_BITS: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
\>(  
    &self,  
    ctx: &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>,  
    inputs: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue")
<F>>  
) -> [SafeType](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/safe_types/struct.SafeType.html "struct halo2_base::safe_types::SafeType")
<F, BYTES\_PER\_ELE, TOTAL\_BITS>

Convert a vector of AssignedValue(treated as little-endian) to a SafeType. The number of bytes of inputs must equal to the number of bytes of outputs. This function also add contraints that a AssignedValue in inputs must be in the range of a byte.

# decompose_bigint_option in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[decompose\_bigint\_option](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose_bigint_option.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#265-271)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn decompose_bigint_option<F: BigPrimeField>(    value: Value<&BigInt>,    number_of_limbs: usize,    bit_len: usize) -> Vec<Value<F>>

Expand description

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
 into `num_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 represented by those limbs wrapped in \[Value\].

Assumes `bit_len` < 128.

*   `e`: immutable reference to `BigInt` to decompose
*   `num_limbs`: number of limbs to decompose `e` into
*   `bit_len`: number of bits in each limb

---

# decompose_biguint in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[decompose\_biguint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose_biguint.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#207-243)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn decompose_biguint<F: BigPrimeField>(    e: &BigUint,    num_limbs: usize,    bit_len: usize) -> Vec<F>

Expand description

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [BigUint](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/biguint/struct.BigUint.html "BigUint")
 into `num_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 represented by those limbs.

Assumes 64 <= `bit_len` < 128.

*   `e`: immutable reference to [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
     to decompose
*   `num_limbs`: number of limbs to decompose `e` into
*   `bit_len`: number of bits in each limb

Truncates to `num_limbs` limbs if `e` is too large.

---

# fe_to_bigint in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[fe\_to\_bigint](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.fe_to_bigint.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#152-161)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn fe_to_bigint<F: BigPrimeField>(fe: &F) -> BigInt

Expand description

Converts a [BigPrimeField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/traitalias.BigPrimeField.html "BigPrimeField")
 element into a [BigInt](https://docs.rs/num-bigint/0.4/num_bigint/bigint/struct.BigInt.html "BigInt")
 element by sending `fe` in `[0, F::modulus())` to\
\
ⓘ\
\
    fe,                 if fe < F::modulus() / 2\
    fe - F::modulus(),  otherwise

---

# decompose_fe_to_u64_limbs in halo2_base::utils - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[decompose\_fe\_to\_u64\_limbs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fn.decompose_fe_to_u64_limbs.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#183-197)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn decompose_fe_to_u64_limbs<F: ScalarField>(    e: &F,    number_of_limbs: usize,    bit_len: usize) -> Vec<u64>

Expand description

Decomposes an immutable reference to a [ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "ScalarField")
 element into `number_of_limbs` limbs of `bit_len` bits each and returns a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html "u64")
 represented by those limbs.

Assumes `bit_len` < 64

*   `e`: immutable reference to [ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "ScalarField")
     element to decompose
*   `number_of_limbs`: number of limbs to decompose `e` into
*   `bit_len`: number of bits in each limb

---

`log2_ceil(0)` returns 0.

::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#276-282)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn value_to_option<V>(value: Value<V>) -> Option<V>

Expand description

Wraps the internal value of `value` in an [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "Option")
. If the value is [None](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html#variant.None "None")
, then the function returns [None](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html#variant.None "None")
.

*   `value`: Value to convert.

    pub struct KeygenAssignments<F: ScalarField> {
        pub assigned_advices: HashMap<(usize, usize), (Cell, usize)>,
        pub assigned_constants: HashMap<F, Cell>,
        pub break_points: MultiPhaseThreadBreakPoints,
    }

Expand description

Stores the cell values loaded during the Keygen phase of a halo2 proof and breakpoints for multi-threading

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

Expand description

Builds the process for gate threading

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#fields)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#structfield.threads)
`threads: [[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <[Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context") <F>>; [3](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.array.html) ]`

Threads for each challenge phase

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#structfield.witness_gen_only)
`witness_gen_only: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html) `

Flag for witness generation. If true, the gate thread builder is used for witness generation only.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#52-365)
[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#impl-GateThreadBuilder%3CF%3E)

### impl<F: [ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "trait halo2_base::utils::ScalarField")
\> [GateThreadBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "struct halo2_base::gates::builder::GateThreadBuilder")
<F>

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#58-63)

#### pub fn [new](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.new)
(witness\_gen\_only: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> Self

Creates a new [GateThreadBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "GateThreadBuilder")
 and spawns a main thread in phase 0.

*   `witness_gen_only`: If true, the [GateThreadBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "GateThreadBuilder")
     is used for witness generation only.
    *   If true, the gate thread builder only does witness asignments and does not store constraint information – this should only be used for the real prover.
    *   If false, the gate thread builder is used for keygen and mock prover (it can also be used for real prover) and the builder stores circuit information (e.g. copy constraints, fixed columns, enabled selectors).
        *   These values are fixed for the circuit at key generation time, and they do not need to be re-computed by the prover in the actual proving phase.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#68-70)

#### pub fn [mock](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.mock)
() -> Self

Creates a new [GateThreadBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "GateThreadBuilder")
 with `witness_gen_only` set to false.

Performs the witness assignment computations and then checks using normal programming logic whether the gate constraints are all satisfied.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#75-77)

#### pub fn [keygen](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.keygen)
() -> Self

Creates a new [GateThreadBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "GateThreadBuilder")
 with `witness_gen_only` set to false.

Performs the witness assignment computations and generates prover and verifier keys.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#82-84)

#### pub fn [prover](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.prover)
() -> Self

Creates a new [GateThreadBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "GateThreadBuilder")
 with `witness_gen_only` set to true.

Performs the witness assignment computations and then runs the proving system.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#88-90)

#### pub fn [unknown](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.unknown)
(self, use\_unknown: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> Self

Creates a new [GateThreadBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "GateThreadBuilder")
 with `use_unknown` flag set.

*   `use_unknown`: If true, during key generation witness \[Value\]s are replaced with Value::unknown() for safety.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#94-100)

#### pub fn [main](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.main)
(&mut self, phase: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>

Returns a mutable reference to the [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 of a gate thread. Spawns a new thread for the given phase, if none exists.

*   `phase`: The challenge phase (as an index) of the gate thread.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#103-105)

Returns the `witness_gen_only` flag.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#108-110)

Returns the `use_unknown` flag.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#113-115)

#### pub fn [thread\_count](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.thread_count)
(&self) -> [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)

Returns the current number of threads in the [GateThreadBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "GateThreadBuilder")
.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#118-122)

#### pub fn [get\_new\_thread\_id](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.get_new_thread_id)
(&mut self) -> [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)

Creates a new thread id by incrementing the `thread count`

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#126-131)

#### pub fn [new\_thread](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.new_thread)
(&mut self, phase: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> &mut [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "struct halo2_base::Context")
<F>

Spawns a new thread for a new given `phase`. Returns a mutable reference to the [Context](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.Context.html "Context")
 of the new thread.

*   `phase`: The phase (index) of the gate thread.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#137-189)

#### pub fn [config](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.config)
(  
    &self,  
    k: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
,  
    minimum\_rows: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
\>  
) -> [FlexGateConfigParams](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.FlexGateConfigParams.html "struct halo2_base::gates::builder::FlexGateConfigParams")

Auto-calculates configuration parameters for the circuit

*   `k`: The number of in the circuit (i.e. numeber of rows = 2k)
*   `minimum_rows`: The minimum number of rows in the circuit that cannot be used for witness assignments and contain random `blinding factors` to ensure zk property, defaults to 0.

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#205-364)

#### pub fn [assign\_all](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.assign_all)
(  
    &self,  
    config: &[FlexGateConfig](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html "struct halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::FlexGateConfig")
<F>,  
    lookup\_advice: &\[[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")\
<Column<Advice>>\],  
    q\_lookup: &\[[Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")\
<Selector>\],  
    region: &mut Region<'\_, F>,  
    \_: [KeygenAssignments](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.KeygenAssignments.html "struct halo2_base::gates::builder::KeygenAssignments")
<F>  
) -> [KeygenAssignments](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.KeygenAssignments.html "struct halo2_base::gates::builder::KeygenAssignments")
<F>

Assigns all advice and fixed cells, turns on selectors, and imposes equality constraints.

Returns the assigned advices, and constants in the form of [KeygenAssignments](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.KeygenAssignments.html "KeygenAssignments")
.

Assumes selector and advice columns are already allocated and of the same length.

Note: `assign_all()` **should** be called during keygen or if using mock prover. It also works for the real prover, but there it is more optimal to use [`assign_threads_in`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/fn.assign_threads_in.html "assign_threads_in")
 instead.

*   `config`: The [FlexGateConfig](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html "FlexGateConfig")
     of the circuit.
*   `lookup_advice`: The lookup advice columns.
*   `q_lookup`: The lookup advice selectors.
*   `region`: The \[Region\] of the circuit.
*   `assigned_advices`: The assigned advice cells.
*   `assigned_constants`: The assigned fixed cells.
*   `break_points`: The break points of the circuit.

const: [unstable](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/88674 "Tracking issue for const_convert")

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html#method.into)

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

    pub struct RangeCircuitBuilder<F: ScalarField>(pub GateCircuitBuilder<F>);

Expand description

A wrapper struct to auto-build a circuit from a `GateThreadBuilder`.

# RangeWithInstanceCircuitBuilder in halo2_base::gates::builder - Rust

Struct [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[gates](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/index.html)
::[builder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/index.html)
::[RangeWithInstanceCircuitBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.RangeWithInstanceCircuitBuilder.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#678-683)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub struct RangeWithInstanceCircuitBuilder<F: ScalarField> {
        pub circuit: RangeCircuitBuilder<F>,
        pub assigned_instances: Vec<AssignedValue<F>>,
    }

Expand description

This is an extension of [`RangeCircuitBuilder`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.RangeCircuitBuilder.html "RangeCircuitBuilder")
 that adds support for public instances (aka public inputs+outputs)

The intended design is that a [`GateThreadBuilder`](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.GateThreadBuilder.html "GateThreadBuilder")
 is populated and then produces some assigned instances, which are supplied as `assigned_instances` to this struct. The \[`Circuit`\] implementation for this struct will then expose these instances and constrain them using the Halo2 API.

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.RangeWithInstanceCircuitBuilder.html#fields)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.RangeWithInstanceCircuitBuilder.html#structfield.circuit)
`circuit: [RangeCircuitBuilder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.RangeCircuitBuilder.html "struct halo2_base::gates::builder::RangeCircuitBuilder") <F>`

The underlying circuit builder

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/struct.RangeWithInstanceCircuitBuilder.html#structfield.assigned_instances)
`assigned_instances: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <[AssignedValue](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/struct.AssignedValue.html "struct halo2_base::AssignedValue") <F>>`

The assigned instances to expose publicly at the end of circuit synthesis

<U> for Twhere

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

    pub struct BasicGateConfig<F: ScalarField> {
        pub q_enable: Selector,
        pub value: Column<Advice>,
        /* private fields */
    }

Expand description

A configuration for a basic gate chip describing the selector, and advice column values.

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.BasicGateConfig.html#fields)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.BasicGateConfig.html#structfield.q_enable)
`q_enable: Selector`

\[Selector\] column that stores selector values that are used to activate gates in the advice column.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.BasicGateConfig.html#structfield.value)
`value: Column<Advice>`

\[Column\] that stores the advice values of the gate.

#### pub fn [configure](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.BasicGateConfig.html#method.configure)
(  
    meta: &mut ConstraintSystem<F>,  
    strategy: [GateStrategy](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/enum.GateStrategy.html "enum halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::GateStrategy")
,  
    phase: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)
  
) -> Self

Instantiates a new [BasicGateConfig](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.BasicGateConfig.html "BasicGateConfig")
.

Assumes `phase` is in the range \[0, MAX\_PHASE).\
\
*   `meta`: \[ConstraintSystem\] used for the gate\
*   `strategy`: The [GateStrategy](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/enum.GateStrategy.html "GateStrategy")\
     to use for the gate\
*   `phase`: The phase to add the gate to\
\

![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#397-401)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn gen_srs(k: u32) -> ParamsKZG<Bn256>

Expand description

Generates the SRS for the KZG scheme and writes it to a file found in “./params/kzg\_bn2\_{k}.srs`or`{dir}/kzg\_bn254\_{k}.srs`if`PARAMS\_DIR\` env var is specified, creates a file it if it does not exist”

*   `k`: degree that expresses the size of circuit (i.e., 2^k is the number of rows in the circuit)

---

    pub struct FlexGateConfig<F: ScalarField> {
        pub basic_gates: [Vec<BasicGateConfig<F>>; 3],
        pub constants: Vec<Column<Fixed>>,
        pub num_advice: [usize; 3],
        pub max_rows: usize,
        /* private fields */
    }

Expand description

Defines a configuration for a flex gate chip describing the selector, and advice column values for the chip.

Fields[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html#fields)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html#structfield.basic_gates)
`basic_gates: [[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <[BasicGateConfig](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.BasicGateConfig.html "struct halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::BasicGateConfig") <F>>; [3](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.array.html) ]`

A [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of [BasicGateConfig](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.BasicGateConfig.html "BasicGateConfig")
 that define gates for each halo2 phase.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html#structfield.constants)
`constants: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <Column<Fixed>>`

A [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of \[Fixed\] \[Column\]s for allocating constant values.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html#structfield.num_advice)
`num_advice: [[usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) ; [3](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.array.html) ]`

Number of advice columns for each halo2 phase.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html#structfield.max_rows)
`max_rows: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

### impl<F: [ScalarField](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/trait.ScalarField.html "trait halo2_base::utils::ScalarField")
\> [FlexGateConfig](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html "struct halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::FlexGateConfig")
<F>

[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate.rs.html#114-153)

#### pub fn [configure](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html#method.configure)
(  
    meta: &mut ConstraintSystem<F>,  
    strategy: [GateStrategy](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/enum.GateStrategy.html "enum halo2_base::gates::flex_gate::GateStrategy")
,  
    num\_advice: &\[[usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)\
\],  
    num\_fixed: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
,  
    circuit\_degree: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
  
) -> Self

Generates a new [FlexGateConfig](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html "FlexGateConfig")

Assumes `num_advice` is a [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
 of length [MAX\_PHASE](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/constant.MAX_PHASE.html "MAX_PHASE")

*   `meta`: \[ConstraintSystem\] of the circuit
*   `strategy`: [GateStrategy](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/enum.GateStrategy.html "GateStrategy")
     of the flex gate
*   `num_advice`: Number of \[Advice\] \[Column\]s in each phase
*   `num_fixed`: Number of \[Fixed\] \[Column\]s in each phase
*   `circuit_degree`: Degree that expresses the size of circuit (i.e., 2^circuit\_degree is the number of rows in the circuit)

Trait Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/struct.FlexGateConfig.html#trait-implementations)

# assign_threads_in in halo2_base::gates::builder - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[gates](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/index.html)
::[builder](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/index.html)
::[assign\_threads\_in](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/builder/fn.assign_threads_in.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/builder.rs.html#380-446)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn assign_threads_in<F: ScalarField>(    phase: usize,    threads: Vec<Context<F>>,    config: &FlexGateConfig<F>,    lookup_advice: &[Column<Advice>],    region: &mut Region<'_, F>,    break_points: ThreadBreakPoints)

Expand description

Assigns threads to regions of advice column.

Uses preprocessed `break_points` to assign where to divide the advice column into a new column for each thread.

Performs only witness generation, so should only be evoked during proving not keygen.

Assumes that the advice columns are already assigned.

*   `phase` - the phase of the circuit
*   `threads` - [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "Vec")
     threads to assign
*   `config` - immutable reference to the configuration of the circuit
*   `lookup_advice` - Slice of lookup advice columns
*   `region` - mutable reference to the region to assign threads to
*   `break_points` - the preprocessed break points for the threads

---

# read_or_create_srs in halo2_base::utils::fs - Rust

Function [halo2\_base](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/index.html)
::[utils](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/index.html)
::[fs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fs/index.html)
::[read\_or\_create\_srs](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/utils/fs/fn.read_or_create_srs.html#)
![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/utils.rs.html#371-393)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub fn read_or_create_srs<'a, C: CurveAffine, P: ParamsProver<'a, C>>(    k: u32,    setup: impl Fn(u32) -> P) -> P

Expand description

Attempts to read the srs from a file found in `./params/kzg_bn254_{k}.srs` or `{dir}/kzg_bn254_{k}.srs` if `PARAMS_DIR` env var is specified, creates a file it if it does not exist.

*   `k`: degree that expresses the size of circuit (i.e., 2^k is the number of rows in the circuit)
*   `setup`: a function that creates the srs

---

![Copy item path](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/clipboard.svg)


[source](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/src/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate.rs.html#24-33)
 · [\[−\]](javascript:void(0) "collapse all docs")

    pub enum GateStrategy {
        Vertical,
    }

Expand description

Specifies the gate strategy for the gate chip

Variants[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/enum.GateStrategy.html#variants)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/enum.GateStrategy.html#variant.Vertical)
`Vertical`

#### [Vertical Gate Strategy:](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/enum.GateStrategy.html#vertical-gate-strategy)

`q_0 * (a + b * c - d) = 0` where

*   a = value\[0\], b = value\[1\], c = value\[2\], d = value\[3\]
*   q = q\_enable\[0\]
*   q is either 0 or 1 so this is just a simple selector We chose `a + b * c` instead of `a * b + c` to allow “chaining” of gates, i.e., the output of one gate because `a` in the next gate.

Trait Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/flex_gate/enum.GateStrategy.html#trait-implementations)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Underlying Gate Configuration

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeConfig.html#structfield.lookup_advice)
`lookup_advice: [[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <Column<Advice>>; [3](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.array.html) ]`

Special advice (witness) Columns used only for lookup tables.

Each phase of a halo2 circuit has a distinct lookup\_advice column.

*   If `gate` has only 1 advice column, lookups are enabled for that column, in which case `lookup_advice` is empty
*   If `gate` has more than 1 advice column some number of user-specified `lookup_advice` columns are added
    *   In this case, we don’t need a selector so `q_lookup` is empty

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeConfig.html#structfield.q_lookup)
`q_lookup: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec") <[Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option") <Selector>>`

Selector values for the lookup table.

[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeConfig.html#structfield.lookup)
`lookup: TableColumn`

Column for lookup table values.

Implementations[](https://axiom-crypto.github.io/halo2-lib/halo2_base/gates/range/struct.RangeConfig.html#implementations)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
